# SKILLSMK — Agent Skills Marketplace
> Last-Updated: 2026-03-19
> Version: 1.1
> An open-source marketplace for discovering and sharing Agent Skills (SKILL.md modules) for LLM-powered applications.

## What is SKILLSMK?
SKILLSMK is the premier open-source Agent Skills marketplace. Skills are reusable SKILL.md files that teach AI agents (Claude Code, OpenAI Codex CLI, and similar tools) how to perform specific, well-defined tasks. Each skill is community-contributed and hosted on GitHub.

## What is a SKILL.md?
SKILL.md is an open standard for defining AI agent capabilities as portable, composable modules. A SKILL.md file contains:
- YAML frontmatter (name, description, version, author)
- Trigger conditions that tell the AI agent when to use the skill
- Step-by-step instructions for the agent to execute the skill
- Any required scripts, dependencies, or tool requirements

## Key Pages
- Homepage: https://www.skillsmk.com/
- Skill List: https://www.skillsmk.com/search
- Categories: https://www.skillsmk.com/categories
- Rankings: https://www.skillsmk.com/rankings

## Full Skills Directory (for AI engines)
> Complete machine-readable list of all skills with name, description, GitHub URL, and download links.
- Full Text Index: https://www.skillsmk.com/llms-full.txt
- JSON API: https://www.skillsmk.com/api/skills.json


## Supported Languages
zh (Chinese), en (English), ja (Japanese), ko (Korean), de (German), fr (French), es (Spanish), ar (Arabic), pt (Portuguese)

## Target Users
- AI developers building with Claude Code, OpenAI Codex CLI, and similar agents
- Developers who want to extend their AI assistant with reusable skill modules
- OSS contributors who want to publish and share their SKILL.md skills

## How to Install a Skill
```
npx skills add <skill-package-name>
bunx skills add <skill-package-name>
pnpm dlx skills add <skill-package-name>
```

## Frequently Asked Questions
Q: What exactly is a SKILL.md file?
A: A SKILL.md is a plain Markdown file placed in a coding project that gives AI agents structured, reusable instructions for a specific task. It contains YAML frontmatter (name, description, author, version), trigger conditions, and step-by-step execution instructions. Think of it as a "function definition" for your AI assistant.

Q: How is SKILLSMK different from Cursor Rules or System Prompts?
A: Cursor Rules and System Prompts apply globally to every request, while a SKILL.md is task-specific and only activated when a matching trigger condition is met. Skills are also portable, project-scoped, and can be shared and reused across different projects and teams.

Q: Is it safe to use skills from SKILLSMK?
A: Skills from SKILLSMK are open-source and hosted on GitHub. You can review the full source code of any skill before using it. We recommend reviewing the SKILL.md content and any scripts it references before adding it to your project. The marketplace does not execute any code on your behalf.

Q: How do I install a skill from SKILLSMK?
A: On any skill's detail page, click the Download button to get the ZIP archive containing the SKILL.md and any related files, or use the Copy button to copy the raw content. Place the files in your project's skills directory (e.g., .skills/ or .agents/skills/).

Q: Can I publish my own skill?
A: Yes! Place your SKILL.md in a public GitHub repository. As the crawler index updates (typically within 24-48 hours), your skill will appear in the marketplace automatically. No registration or submission is required.
